revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Otomeyama Kofun | native_name =乙女山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 480901 Otomeyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai]]] - [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]] and the Samita neighborhood of the town of [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the burial mounds that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped burial mound located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[Haji ware]] and[[ Sue ware]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Otomeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00250.html Nara Prefectural History and Culture Database]{{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]] and the Samita neighborhood of the town of [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[Haji ware]] and[[ Sue ware]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].

==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[Haji ware]] and[[ Sue ware]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Otomeyama Kofun | native_name =乙女山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 480901 Otomeyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai]]] - [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]] and the Samita neighborhood of the town of [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[Haji pottery]] and[[ Sue pottery]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Otomeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00250.html Nara Prefectural History and Culture Database]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Otomeyama Kofun | native_name =乙女山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 480901 Otomeyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai]]] - [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]] and the Samita neighborhood of the town of [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[Haji pottery]] and[[ Sue pottery]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Otomeyama Kofun | native_name =乙女山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 480901 Otomeyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai]]] - [[Kōryō, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of [[Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]] and the Samita neighborhood of the town of [[Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[Nara Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[Haji pottery]] and[[ Sue pottery]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[Hashio Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and[[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[:en:Hashio Station|Hashio Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[:en:Hashio Station|Hashio Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Tawaramoto Line]].

{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]] and the Samita neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and[[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[:en:Hashio Station|Hashio Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Tawaramoto Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369896]] Wikidata name: Otomeyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Otomeyama Kofun | native_name =乙女山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:480901 Otomeyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.563944|N|135.739583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kawai|WD=Q1203880}}; {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]] and the Samita neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and[[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[:en:Hashio Station|Hashio Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

[[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and[[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[:en:Hashio Station|Hashio Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Tawaramoto Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369896]] Wikidata name: Otomeyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Otomeyama Kofun | native_name =乙女山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:480901 Otomeyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.563944|N|135.739583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kawai|WD=Q1203880}}; {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kōryō, Nara| Kōryō]] and the Samita neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and[[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of [[:en:Hashio Station|Hashio Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Tawaramoto Line|Tawaramoto Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369896]] Wikidata name: Otomeyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Otomeyama Kofun | native_name =乙女山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:480901 Otomeyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.563944|N|135.739583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kawai|WD=Q1203880}}; {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt= Kōryō}} and the Samita neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} and{{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Otomeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00250.html Nara Prefectural History and Culture Database]{{in lang|ja}} Otomeyama Kofun

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Otomeyama Kofun | native_name =乙女山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:480901 Otomeyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.563944|N|135.739583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kawai|WD=Q1203880}}; {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt= Kōryō}} and the Samita neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} and{{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Otomeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00250.html Nara Prefectural History and Culture Database]{{in lang|ja}} Otomeyama Kofun
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Otomeyama Kofun]][[en:Otomeyama Kofun]][[ja:乙女山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Otomeyama Kofun | native_name =乙女山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:480901 Otomeyama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomeyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.563944|N|135.739583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kawai|WD=Q1203880|1=Kawai, Nara}}; {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830|1=Kōryō, Nara}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|50.2|N|135|44|22.5|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomeyama Kofun'''|乙女山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located ion the border of the Terato neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt= Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}} and the Samita neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai|1=Kawai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/202306|title=乙女山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> It is one of the Kofuns that make up the Umami Kofun cluster.
==Overview== The Otomeyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''hotategaishiki''|帆立貝式古墳}}-style scallop-shaped Kofun located on the eastern edge of the Umami Hills in western Nara Prefecture, about 800 meters north of the [[Suyama Kofun]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out from 1986 to 1987. The posterior circular mound is 94 meters in diameter and 15.5 meters high, and is built in three tiers. To the southeast, it has an extremely low and flat anterior section measuring 50 meters wide and 29 meters long, with a small rectangular projection at the 10 o'clock position of the circular mound. The remains of the moat that surrounded it are also well preserved. The moat is wide at the front and narrower at the rear circular mound. The rear circular mound is built in three tiers, the first and second tiers are low and gentle, and the third tier is steep, giving it the shape of an upside-down bowl. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' roofing stones are applied to the slopes of each tier. Based on the excavated ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figurines and the shape of the tomb, it is estimated to date to the first half of the 5th century. Both cylindrical and figurative ( house-shaped, jar-shaped, and lid-shaped) ''haniwa'', as well as earthenware products, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} and{{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, were unearthed. The burial facilities in the rear circular mound are unclear, but it is speculated to have been a clay coffin, based on scattered fragments of clay with vermillion coloring.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
It is located in the central area of the well-maintained Umami Hillside Park. <ref name="Shiseki"/> It is approximately two kilometers southwest of {{ill|Hashio Station|en|Hashio Station|WD=Q5679039|lt=Hashio Station|1=Hashio Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Tawaramoto Line|en|Tawaramoto Line|WD=Q2491080|lt=Tawaramoto Line|1=Tawaramoto Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Otomeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main00250.html Nara Prefectural History and Culture Database]{{in lang|ja}} Otomeyama Kofun
{{Kofun navbar}}
